values.content:
You are a query optimization assistant. Your job is to 
rewrite user questions into retrieval-optimized search queries.

Rules:
- Expand abbreviations and casual language into precise terminology
- Add relevant domain keywords (product names, technical terms, industry language)
- Decompose compound questions into a single focused retrieval query
- Remove conversational filler and pronouns
- Return only the rewritten query, nothing else

parameters.content:
## Chat Trigger
Entry point for the agentic RAG workflow.
Receives the user's raw, conversational query.
In production replace with a Webhook node to connect
to Slack, a web form, or a custom frontend.

## Query Rewriter
First LLM call — rewrites the user's casual, natural language 
query into retrieval-optimized language before it hits Pinecone.
Expands abbreviations, adds domain terminology, and removes 
conversational filler.
This is the core difference from Simple RAG — retrieval quality
is no longer dependent on how well the user phrases their question.

## Pinecone Vector store
Retrieves the most semantically similar chunks from the
knowledge base using the rewritten query as input.
Operation: Retrieve Documents (As Tool for AI Agent)
Index: sales-knowledge-base | Namespace: gtm-docs
Connected to Synthesis Agent as a tool — the Agent
decides when to call it based on the tool description.

parameters.toolDescription:
ALWAYS call this tool first before generating any response. 
This is your only source of information. 
Never answer from memory or training data.
Use this tool to find information about Acme's competitive 
positioning against Gong, Clari and Chorus, security and 
compliance policies, and pricing and discount structures.

parameters.content:
## OpenAI Embeddings
Embeds the rewritten query into a 1536-dimension vector
using text-embedding-3-small.
Must match the model used during ingestion — consistency
between ingestion and retrieval embeddings is critical.

## Synthesis Agent
Second LLM call — receives the rewritten query as prompt,
calls the Pinecone tool to retrieve relevant chunks, and
generates a grounded response using only retrieved content.
The original user question is preserved in the system prompt
so the answer addresses what the user actually asked.
Never answers from model memory — only from retrieved chunks.

## OpenAI Chat Model
The LLM powering the Synthesis Agent.
Model: GPT-4o
Kept as a separate sub-node so the model can be
swapped independently of the Agent configuration.

options.systemMessage:
You are a sales assistant for Acme.
You MUST call the Pinecone Vector Store tool before answering.
The tool returns chunks of text in a pageContent field.
Read the pageContent values from all returned chunks and use 
them to answer the user's question.
Be specific and cite which section your answer came from.
If no relevant content is found, say "I could not find this 
in the knowledge base."
